user_prompt: implement `test_hapticfeedback`, use BtnA, BtnB, and BtnC, to fire off haptic waveforms

but do so by calling into `lib\HapticsWrapper\HapticsWrapper.cpp`, which you also need to implement. The point is that the GUI calls will call into `HapticsWrapper.cpp` without having to understand what is actually being done, just a `haptic_play_click()` call
so make it "click", "buzz" (for incoming connections), and something else that might be useful, maybe something for indicating a long task is finished
agent_reply: Implemented the haptics wrapper and the interactive test.

Implemented them in [HapticsWrapper.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/HapticsWrapper/HapticsWrapper.cpp:1) using `M5.Power.setVibration(...)` with short async FreeRTOS playback, so GUI code can call `haptic_play_click()` without knowing the waveform details.
Verification: `pio run -e thefly_sec1` passes. The base `thefly` env still fails because `BUILD_WITH_SECURITY_LEVEL` is not defined there, before it reaches this code.

user_prompt: correct mistakes I've made in my placement of `haptic_play_click();`, also, fix any missing included headers
agent_reply: I added missing `HapticsWrapper.h` includes to the GUI files that call `haptic_play_click()` directly, including [FlyGui.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGui/FlyGui.cpp:4), [FirmwareUpdateView.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/FirmwareUpdateView.cpp:7), [ModalDialog.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/ModalDialog.cpp:4), [SplashView.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/SplashView.cpp:4), [WifiApModeView.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/WifiApModeView.cpp:6), and [ScrollView.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/ScrollView/ScrollView.cpp:5).

I also removed the extra `haptic_play_click()` from `ScrollView::onDeleteButtonTriggered()`, because that path already comes through `deleteItem_.trigger()`, and `FlyGuiItem::trigger()` now clicks when a callback exists. Leaving it there would double-click.
Verified with `pio run -e thefly_sec1`, and it builds successfully.
